note:
据Cemetech报道，德州仪器已经取消了一些最受欢迎的计算器运行汇编语言或C语言程序的能力。业余爱好者对这些变化并不满意。除了被用作教育工具外，TI计算器也是相对简单和廉价的编程设备。
根据发布在TI-Planet(通过Linus Tech Tips)上的一封客户支持电子邮件，受影响的计算器包括流行的TI-84 Plus CE，以及TI-83 Plus CE-T和TI-83 Premium CE。在设备的最新固件更新中，该功能正在消失，目前无法回滚软件。
这一改变是为了阻止学生绕过计算器的考试模式限制，但在这个过程中，它也移除了业余爱好者用来运行程序的一个关键途径。虽然计算器仍然支持其他编程语言，如TI-Basic和Python，但据报道，这些语言的速度要慢得多，这意味着什么类型的程序可以合理运行。
TI的狂热分子争辩说，坚决作弊的人不会因为这一变化而停止。例如，Planet Casio上的一个论坛帖子指出，人们仍然可以修改计算器的考试模式通知LED。考试主持人也很少检查学生是否在计算器上运行最新的固件，所以理论上作弊的人可以运行较旧的固件版本作为解决办法。
没有人强迫业余爱好者升级他们的固件，所以看起来很多人可能会尽可能长时间地保留他们过时的软件。然而，这些变化对整个社区来说是一个巨大的打击，他们将无法再购买支持功能更强大的编程语言的新计算器。
note_en:
Texas Instruments has removed the ability for some of its most popular calculators to run programs written in assembly or C,   Cemetech reports. Hobbyists are not happy with the changes. In addition to being used as educational tools, TI calculators are also relatively simple and cheap programming devices.
 According to a customer support email  posted on  TI-Planet (via   Linus Tech Tips), affected calculators include the popular TI-84 Plus CE, as well as the TI-83 Plus CE-T, and the TI-83 Premium CE. The functionality is disappearing in the devices’ latest firmware update, and there’s currently no way to roll back the software.
  The change is being made to try and stop students from circumventing the calculators’ exam mode restrictions, but in the process it also removes a key avenue that hobbyists have used to run their programs. Although the calculators still support other programming languages like TI-BASIC and Python, these languages are  reportedly far slower, with implications for what kinds of programs can reasonably be run.
 TI enthusiasts contend that determined cheaters won’t be stopped by the change. One forum poster  on  Planet Casio points out that people can still modify the calculators’ Exam Mode notification LED, for example. It’s also rare for exam moderators to check that students are running the most recent firmware on their calculators, so cheaters could theoretically run an older firmware version as a workaround.
 No one is forcing hobbyists to upgrade their firmware, so it seems likely that many will hang on to their outdated software for as long as possible. However, the changes are a big blow to the community as a whole, which will no longer be able to buy new calculators with support for the more powerful programming languages.
title: 德州仪器限制计算器编程支持
title_en: Texas Instruments limits calculator programming support

note:
HBO Max是一家以HBO为基础的流媒体服务，充斥着额外的节目和电影，经过几个月的炒作，于周三推出。它的到来为一些相当简单的问题带来了一个复杂的答案矩阵：它将花费我多少钱？我可以在哪里播放呢？
与Netflix和迪士尼+(Disney Plus)的竞争对手一样，HBO Max拥有庞大的热门节目和电影目录，以及充斥着明星的大预算独家原创节目名单。但HBO Max是其中最贵的。新订户可以注册，在为期一周的免费试用后，每月只需支付15美元的订阅费，这与HBO对大多数付费电视提供商的线性频道和之前存在的独立流媒体服务HBO Now收取的价格相同。
但是如果你已经在以某种形式支付HBO的费用，那就是你现在必须为Max支付的金额，或者你是否必须支付任何额外的费用。嗯，这很复杂。
问题是：你们的供应商必须为MAX与我们打交道吗？你们会搬过去吗？华纳媒体流媒体业务总经理安迪·福塞尔(Andy Forssell)上周在接受采访时表示，答案将相当简单，除此之外，它还会变得有点复杂。我们有非常广泛的经销渠道，而且……。下周二午夜，我们将在原地踏步--并不是说这就是终点，如果有任何讨论没有完成的话。
为了吸引你试一试，HBO Max给自己添加了比你现在在普通HBO频道或HBO上看到的更多的内容。同样的价格，HBO Max使你可以观看的节目数量翻了一番。AT&amp；T拥有HBO，作为其旗下华纳媒体(WarnerMedia)电视和电影部门的一部分。该公司一直在原创节目预算中投入数十亿美元，并与DC、哈利波特(Harry Potter)和吉卜力工作室(Studio Ghibli)达成昂贵的协议，播放DC、哈利波特(Harry Potter)和吉卜力工作室(Studio Ghibli)的大片，以及“老友记”(Friends)、“生活大爆炸”(The Big Bang)和“里克与莫蒂”(。(本文接近尾声时，将深入探讨Max上的节目和电影。)。
为了更容易地找到这些知名的特许经营权，HBO Max应用程序有许多基于它们的中心，所以你可以快速进入HBO的节目、DC或Ghibli；s。与Netflix以数据为导向的推荐相比，HBO Max应用程序还采取了更精心策划的方式来展示值得观看的内容。HBO Max希望它的应用程序设计也能与Netflix截然不同。作为占主导地位的流媒体服务，Netflix的用户界面成为设计订阅视频应用的黄金标准。但HBO Max在其UI中尝试了不同的形状--景观风格的矩形代表一集，正方形代表你的观看历史的个性化，圆圈引入人的元素，比如围绕特定演员或角色的推荐。
华纳传媒(WarnerMedia)执行副总裁兼直接面向消费者业务总经理福塞尔(Forssell)表示，我们的出发点是视觉多样性。然后第二件事是：让人类参与到管理中来。
HBO Max宣布的最新高调独家电影是传说中的2017年电影“正义联盟”中的斯奈德·卡通(Snyder Cut)。这部由导演扎克·斯奈德(Zack Snyder)制作的DC电影的候补剪辑耗资超过3000万美元，将于2021年在该服务上进行流媒体播放。除此之外，HBO Max的所谓Max Originals将提供其他任何地方都买不到的新节目和电影。推出时，这项服务将有六个，包括由安娜·肯德里克(Anna Kendrick)主演的爱情选集系列，每集都会跟随她的角色经历一段新的浪漫关系。HBO Max今年夏天还将增加11部原创电影，其中包括塞斯·罗根(Seth Rogen)的喜剧电影“美国泡菜”(An American Pickle)8月6日的首映式。
但一款备受期待的原版在发射时就不见了。“老友记”聚会特辑计划在第一天成为这项服务的独家招牌。但就像世界上的大多数东西一样，HBO Max的原创作品也被冠状病毒大流行扰乱了。
由于被限制在家里，全国各地的人们正在播放更多的视频，但新型冠状病毒及其呼吸道疾病新冠肺炎也关闭了全球的电视制作。HBO Max表示，它将交付计划推出的95%的新节目和电影，并很快推出。但“老友记”重聚特别节目不在其中。该公司正在重现这部情景喜剧，定于3月初在现场观众面前主持其六位明星，当时封锁和社会距离使该项目在可预见的未来被搁置。
HBO Max每月15美元的价格相当于现在HBO和HBO传统频道的费用，当你从大多数付费电视提供商那里获得它的时候。相比之下，迪士尼Plus的月费是7美元，Netflix的最便宜档次是9美元。
对于HBO的全新客户来说，定价很简单。你只需签约，并支付每月15美元的费用。
但如果你已经为HBO付费了，你是否能免费升级到HBO Max的问题没有简单的答案。一些已经定期订阅HBO或HBO的人现在可以免费获得MAX-但不是每个人。
为了让你自动升级到Max，HBO需要与已经向你收费的公司达成协议，无论是Charge这样的有线电视公司还是苹果这样的数字提供商。HBO Max一直在加大这类交易的力度，越来越多的提供商让他们的HBO客户升级到Max。(请参阅下面的完整列表。)。
但HBO Max并不是与每一家提供商都有交易。例如，康卡斯特是美国最大的有线电视公司，而HBO Max尚未宣布与其基于Xfinity的任何HBO客户达成交易。如果你在这条船上，你可能会被迫选择：要么付两次钱--一次是普通HBO，另一次是HBO Max--或者取消你目前的HBO订阅，重新注册Max。
最重要的是，HBO Max在一些最重要的设备上也无法使用。Roku和Amazon Fire TV都没有被证实拥有HBO Max应用--它们加起来占美国流媒体设备的70%，令人瞠目结舌。如果HBO Max的用户有点像Netflix的流媒体用户，那么绝大多数的观看时间都将在电视上。问问Quibi，当消费者可以在电视上播放大成本节目时，你是否想感受一下他们的感受。
所以，是的，这很复杂。以下是关于HBO Max的成本、折扣、应用程序及其节目和电影的更详细信息。
在第一年的某个时候，这项服务将扩大到包括另一个承载广告的层级。该公司没有具体说明这一级别的价格、发布日期或目录限制，但它可能会更便宜，甚至是免费的。
在HBOMax.com预订这项服务的人解锁了一项折扣协议，第一年的价格降至每月13美元。预订的账户没有资格享受7天的免费试用。
HBO目前独立的流媒体服务HBO Now的月费也是15美元。虽然HBO现在将继续运营，但HBO Max将从本质上取代它。
HBO现在欢迎订户继续使用这项服务，但他们基本上会为较少的节目支付相同的费用。HBO Max现在将在HBO上播放所有相同的节目和电影，外加一大批授权节目和独家原创节目。
与HBO Max之前推出的竞争对手流媒体服务相比，对许多消费者来说，这样的定价可能会感到过高。迪士尼Plus的月费是7美元，Apple TV Plus的月费是5美元。这两家公司都在提供可以进一步降价的优惠，或者为延长一年的自由期提供机会。例如，苹果公司将向从9月初开始购买苹果设备的任何人赠送一年的订阅费。迪士尼还与Verizon达成了一项协议，任何运营商的移动用户都可以免费使用一年。
在那之后，HBO Max的第一批国际扩张将优先考虑拉丁美洲和欧洲。该公司表示，拉丁美洲将在2021年获得HBO Max。
HBO Max在美国的时间安排将使其在一系列流媒体服务发布活动中接近尾声。迪士尼Plus和Apple TV Plus于11月推出。四月带来了Quibi，这是一项仅限移动设备的流媒体服务，以短小、插曲的原创为特色。同样在4月份，NBC环球旗下的孔雀(Peacock)在康卡斯特(Comcast)上对其服务进行了先睹为快。孔雀的全国首发定于7月15日。
HBO Max将在各种设备上使用，包括手机、平板电脑、网络、联网电视和游戏机。
但有两款关键的流媒体设备在发布时并没有HBO Max应用程序。Roku和Amazon Fire电视设备都不支持HBO Max，尽管这些设备代表了美国绝大多数流媒体设备。研究人员Parks Associates估计，美国安装的流媒体电视设备中，近70%要么是Roku，要么是亚马逊Fire电视产品。
5月初，美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)即将上任的首席执行官约翰·斯坦基(John Stankey)警告称，亚马逊的Fire TV产品可能没有HBO Max应用程序。我很高兴地告诉大家，我们将出现在几乎所有的应用商店中，也可能只有一个例外。他说：看起来，当这一切都说完、做完的时候，我们可能已经不在亚马逊Fire应用商店了。
大多数可以流媒体视频的苹果设备都将支持HBO Max--iPhone、iPad、iPod Touch、Apple TV 4K和Apple TV HD。然而，拥有第二代和第三代Apple TV机型的客户将需要使用AirPlay将HBO Max内容从他们的iPhone或iPad流式传输到他们的电视上。
谷歌手机和平板电脑的Android操作系统将会有一款应用程序。Android电视设备、Chromebook、Google Chromecast和Chromecast内置设备也将支持HBO Max。
索尼的PlayStation4系统将可以通过PlayStation商店下载HBO Max应用程序。
微软的Xbox One游戏机也可以通过微软商店获得HBO Max应用。
部分三星联网智能电视-从2016年到2020年的型号-将能够直接下载和购买HBO Max。
在任何人期待已久的HBO Go流媒体功能中，HBO Max为其移动应用程序提供了下载。不幸的是，就像HBO Max的大多数事情一样，对于你可以下载的节目数量是否有限制，该公司没有明确的答案。
福塞尔说：关于允许多少次下载，我们没有明确的答案，但对于大多数人来说，这将是一个非常舒适的数字。限制可能是您的设备中有多少空间与我们的限制相比，随着时间的推移，这将会放开。
HBO Max允许您同时流式传输到三个不同的设备。相比之下，迪士尼Plus允许同时播放四个流，Netflix允许一个到四个流，具体取决于你每月支付的费用。
您还可以创建多个配置文件，以将针对不同家庭成员的推荐分开保存，并且它还具有通过输入PIN即可解锁的儿童帐户。这些家长控制允许父母根据PG和TV-14等收视率，设置每个孩子可以访问的节目的成熟度级别。
对于HBO Now会员，HBO Max通过软件更新自动更换HBO Now应用程序。
但是，从HBO Now和HBO Go到HBO Max的过渡有一个不利之处：如果你从现有的HBO流媒体账户切换，HBO Max Won不会保留你的任何观看历史记录。因此，你的个性化推荐从零开始，HBO Max赢了，例如，你是否已经把“权力的游戏”的每一集都看了三遍，或者你是否已经看了一半电影“小丑”，然后又想从你停止的地方继续看下去。
获得HBO Max最简单的方式就是直接在Homax.com上注册。
通过苹果，HBO Max将成为iPhone和iPad的iOS应用程序，这将允许你注册并通过应用程序内购买进行支付。苹果还将把HBO Max整合到自己的电视应用程序中，允许你将某些流媒体频道聚集在一个地方观看。
通过谷歌，HBO Max将在Google Play商店中拥有一款适用于Android手机和平板电脑的应用程序，你可以在该应用程序中进行应用内购买注册。你还可以在Android电视、Chromebook和谷歌Chromecast上观看HBO Max。如果您现在已经通过Google Play订阅了HBO，您将自动在启动时免费登录HBO Max，并且您将继续通过Google付费。谷歌旗下YouTube运营的流媒体付费电视服务YouTube TV也将播放HBO Max。
拥有支持HBO Max应用程序的三星智能电视的人也可以直接在那里注册。
如果你已经有了HBO，想知道HBO Max的推出对你意味着什么，事情就会变得更加复杂。一些已经订阅了HBO的人可以免费解锁HBO Max，包括但不是所有人。
对于任何现在通过苹果或谷歌订阅HBO的人来说，这里有一些好消息：你几乎不需要做任何事情就可以解锁HBO Max。您的HBO Now应用程序应该会通过支持HBO Max的设备上的软件更新自动被HBO Max应用程序替换。在这种情况下，根据HBO Now Support页面的信息，当你打开新的HBO Max应用程序时，你只需同意HBO Max使用条款和隐私政策，并选择访问所有HBO Max，然后就可以重新设置。
一些HBO Max分销商为他们目前的HBO和HBO Now客户免费解锁了HBO Max升级，这要归功于HBO Max与他们达成的交易。如果您已通过以下方式支付HBO费用，则有资格免费升级HBO MAX：
AT&amp；T(HBO的母公司)和DirecTV、U-Verse、AT&amp；T TV Now、AT&amp；T TV和AT&amp；T无线用户，他们可以添加具有某些无限制计划的HBO。
由独立有线电视和宽带公司组成的NCTC集团--包括WOW！、大西洋宽带、RCN、Grande Communications&Amp；Wave、MCTV和其他公司。(检查您的付费电视提供商是否为NCTC成员。)。
然而，目前还不清楚HBO Max是否会与其他付费电视提供商达成更多协议，无论是康卡斯特(Comcast)或菜谱网络(Dish Network)等传统提供商，还是亚马逊Prime Video频道和Roku Channel等基于互联网的提供商。
美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)还为HBO Max提供了一系列复杂的促销活动。一些客户免费获得MAX，而另一些客户则有资格获得一个月、三个月或一年的免费试用。要弄清你是否有资格，最好的办法是检查你的账户或Myat&amp；T应用程序，但CNET也列出了AT&amp；T&T；在其HBO Max促销活动中的资格条款。
HBO Max包括HBO的所有节目和电影，外加一些备受瞩目的授权内容，如“老友记”、“生活大爆炸”、“里克·莫蒂”和“南方公园”。除此之外，它还会提供独家原创节目。HBO Max在发布时表示，它将拥有10000小时的内容。
最初，HBO Max计划在第一年拥有31个原创节目，第二年增加到50个原创节目。但由于冠状病毒的干扰，这些计划可能会调整。
目录中最容易理解的部分是HBO本身。HBO Max包括常规HBO的所有内容，也就是该频道来来去去的所有戏剧电影；所有HBO电视剧的新剧集，比如“西部世界”(Westworld)和“上周与约翰·奥利弗的今夜秀”(Last Week Tonight With John Oliver)；这些节目的过去几季，以及HBO的完整目录，如“权力的游戏”(Game Of Thrones)、“硅谷”(Silicon Valley)、“副总统”(Veep)和“火线”(The Wire)；以及HBO的任何原创电影、纪录片和特辑。
电影是目录中至关重要的一部分。任何可以在传统HBO网络上观看的电影都可以在HBO Max上进行流媒体播放。HBO MAX电影目录的亮点将包括(但不一定在发布时提供)：
所谓的Snyder Cut of Justice League，定于2021年在HBO Max上播出。
“霍比特人”、“指环王”和“黑客帝国”三部曲的全套。
DC电影，如小丑，神奇女侠，自杀小队，沙扎姆，海王，以及过去40年来每一部蝙蝠侠和超人电影。
吉卜力动画制片厂之前从未在美国上映过流媒体电影。
美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T；)旗下专注于动漫的流媒体服务公司Crunchyroll选择了一些动漫精选，如“富金属炼金师：兄弟情谊”(FullMetal AlChemist：Brotherhood)和其他顶级游戏。
经典电影有“绿野仙踪”、“卡萨布兰卡”、“公民凯恩”、“乱世佳人”、“闪耀”、“雨中之歌”，“2001：太空漫游”、“小精灵”、“银翼杀手：终极剪辑”、“榆树街的噩梦”系列等等。
最重要的是，美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)一直在授权非热门节目，以充实HBO Max&39；的节目目录。最令人瞠目结舌的交易是：
据报道，“老友记”全集的成交价为4.25亿美元。“老友记”一上线就退出Netflix，开始在HBO Max上进行流媒体播放。
它还获得了过去几季“里克与莫蒂”、“神秘博士”最新11季以及未来三季的合同；CW电视剧，如“漂亮小骗子”、“蝙蝠侠”和“Riverdale”衍生剧凯蒂·基恩，以及更多即将上映的CW电视剧；“贝莱尔新鲜王子”；“白宫风云”；以及路德和里奇·热尔维斯等英国电视剧“原创办公室”等。
讽刺动画片“南方公园”不仅将向HBO Max贡献其完整的过往目录，还将播放新的剧集。“南方公园”的最新剧集将在未来三季在喜剧中心播出24小时后在HBO Max上播出。
HBO Max将有独家新剧和名为Max Originals的电影。在很大程度上，HBO Max的原创电视剧将每周删除新的剧集。
“爱情人生”，由安娜·肯德里克主演，肯德里克和保罗·费格制作的浪漫喜剧，因“伴娘”等轰动一时的喜剧而闻名。
Esme&Amp；Roy，芝麻工作室的学龄前动画系列第二季--6月25日。
搜索派对，这部最初在TBS播出的喜剧惊悚片第三季--6月25日。
冒险时间：遥远的土地-蒙特利尔银行，这是复活卡通网络的特许经营冒险时间四个特别节目的第一个-6月25日。
“期待艾米”，这是一部关于喜剧演员艾米·舒默怀孕期间生活的三集纪录片--7月9日。
“寻找”是卡通网络工作室的一部儿童动画系列片，讲述的是一个8岁的孩子和他的小玩意儿猫的故事--7月23日。“寻找”是卡通网络工作室的一部儿童动画系列片，讲述的是一个8岁的孩子和他的小玩意儿猫的故事。
7月30日，一部有剧本的喜剧讲述了一个富有的伦敦人回到她的澳大利亚家乡的故事。
8月6日，赛斯·罗根执导的喜剧“美国泡菜”是HBO Max的第一部原创电影，讲述了一个20世纪20年代的移民在泡菜盐水中保存了100年，并在现代布鲁克林遇到了他的曾孙(两个人物都由罗根饰演)。
然后，在秋季及以后，HBO Max将添加其他原版，包括：
“空乘”，由卡利·库科主演并执行制作，根据克里斯·博贾里安的小说改编。
由狼队从导演兼执行制片人雷德利·斯科特手中提出的科幻史诗。这部10集的电视剧集中讲述了两个机器人在一个神秘的星球上抚养人类孩子的故事。这部剧已经结束了在南非的制作。
HBO Max最初计划在推出年份推出31部原创电视剧(结合HBO内容，总共有69部电视剧)。然后在2021年，HBO Max计划将Max Originals扩大到50部。大约一半的目标人群是16岁至34岁的年轻人，其余的将在HBO品牌以外的更多成人节目和儿童以及家庭内容之间分配。

note_en:
HBO Max, the  HBO-anchored streaming service packed with extra shows and movies,   launched Wednesday after months of hype. Its arrival brings a complex matrix of answers to some pretty simple questions: How much will it cost me? And where can I stream it?
 Like rivals  Netflix and   Disney Plus,  HBO Max has a sprawling catalog of hit shows and movies, plus a big-budget slate of exclusive originals packed with stars. But   HBO Max is the most expensive of the bunch. New subscribers can sign up and pay a simple $15 a month subscription after a week-long free trial, the same price HBO already charges for its linear channel on most pay-TV providers and for its preexisting standalone streaming service, HBO Now.
 But if you're already paying for HBO in some form, the amount you'll have to pay for Max now, or whether you have to pay anything extra at all... well, it's complicated.
 "The question is: Does your provider have to deal with us for Max and do you move over? That answer will be fairly simple, and then beyond that it gets a little more complicated," Andy Forssell, the general manager of WarnerMedia's streaming operation, said in an interview last week. "We've got really broad distribution, and ... midnight next Tuesday we'll be where we are -- not that that's an end point, if there any discussions undone."
 To entice you to give it a try, HBO Max has padded itself with more content than you'll find on either the regular HBO channel or HBO Now. For the same price, HBO Max doubles the amount of programming you can watch. AT&T, which owns HBO as part of its  WarnerMedia TV-and-film division, has been pouring billions of dollars into its budgets for original programming and into expensive deals to stream blockbuster movies from   DC,   Harry Potter and  Studio Ghibli as well as popular shows like  Friends,  The Big Bang Theory and  Rick and Morty. (A deep dive into the shows and movies on Max is near the end of this article.)
 To make it easier to find these well-known franchises, the HBO Max app has hubs based on many of them, so you can quickly jump into HBO's programming, DC's or Ghibli's. It's also taking a more curated approach to surfacing things to watch compared with Netflix's data-driven recommendations. And HBO Max wants it's app design to feel distinct from Netflix too. As the dominant streaming service, Netflix's user interface became the gold standard for designing a subscription video app. But HBO Max is experimenting with different shapes in its UI -- a landscape-style rectangle represents a single episode, a square is something personalized to your watch history and circles lead into a human element, like recommendations around a particular actor or character.
 "The starting point is visual variety," said Forssell, who executive vice president and the general manager of WarnerMedia's direct-to-consumer business. "And then the second piece is: Let's get humans involved in curation."
  The latest high-profile exclusive HBO Max announced is the fabled   "Snyder cut" of the 2017 movie Justice League. Costing more than $30 million to remake, this alternate edit of the DC movie by director Zack Snyder will stream on the service in 2021. Beyond that, HBO Max's so-called Max Originals will offer new shows and movies unavailable anywhere else. At launch, the service will have six, including an  anthology series called Love Life starring Anna Kendrick that follows her character through a new romantic relationship each episode. HBO Max will add 11 more originals over the summer, including the Aug. 6 premiere of Seth Rogen's comedy film An American Pickle.
 But one highly anticipated original is missing at launch. A Friends reunion special was planned to be  the service's marquee exclusive on day one. But like most things in the world, HBO Max's original productions have been disrupted by the coronavirus pandemic.
 Confined to their homes, people across the nation are streaming much more, but the novel coronavirus and its respiratory disease,   COVID-19, have also shut down television productions globally. HBO Max has said it will be delivering 95% of the new shows and movies it planned for launch and soon thereafter. But the  Friends reunion special isn't one of them. The company was in the midst of recreating the sitcom's set to host its six stars in front of a live audience in early March, when lockdowns and social distancing put the project on ice for the foreseeable future.
 HBO Max's $15-a-month price matches the cost of HBO Now and for HBO's traditional channel when you get it through most pay-TV providers. By comparison, Disney Plus is $7 a month and Netflix's cheapest tier is $9.
 For brand new HBO customers, pricing is straightforward. You just sign up, and pay the $15 monthly cost.
 But if you already pay for HBO, the question of whether you get a free upgrade to HBO Max has no simple answer. Some people who already have a regular HBO subscription or HBO Now will get   Max for no extra cost -- but not everyone.
  In order for you to get the automatic upgrade to Max, HBO needs to strike a deal with the company that's already billing you for HBO, whether it's a cable-TV company like Charter or a digital provider like Apple. HBO Max has been ramping up these deals, and there's a growing list of providers that are letting their HBO customers upgrade to Max. (See the full list below.)
 But HBO Max doesn't have deals with every provider. Comcast, for example, is the biggest cable company in the country, and HBO Max hasn't announced a deal for any of its Xfinity-based HBO customers yet. If you're in that boat, you could be forced to choose: either pay twice -- one bill for regular HBO and another for HBO Max -- or cancel your current HBO subscription to re-sign up for Max.
 On top of that, HBO Max isn't available on some of the most important devices either. Neither Roku nor Amazon Fire TV have been confirmed to have an HBO Max app -- together, they represent an eye-popping  70% of the streaming devices in the country. And if HBO Max users are anything like people who stream Netflix, the vast majority of viewing hours will be on televisions.   Ask Quibi if you want a sense of how consumers feel when they can't stream big-budget programming to their TVs.
 So yeah, it's complicated. Below are the finer details about HBO Max's cost, discounts, app and its shows and movies.
    Sometime within the first year, the service will expand to include another tier that carries advertising. The company hasn't specified the price, launch date or catalog limitations on this tier, but it would presumably be cheaper or even free.
  People who preordered the service at HBOMax.com unlocked a discount deal that lowered the price to $13 a month for the first year. Preordered accounts don't qualify for the seven-day free trial.
 HBO's current standalone streaming service, HBO Now, also costs $15 a month. Though HBO Now will continue to operate, HBO Max will essentially replace it.
 HBO Now subscribers are welcome to stick with that service, but they'll essentially be paying the same amount for less programming. HBO Max will have all the same shows and movies on HBO Now plus a big catalog of licensed programming and exclusive originals.
 Compared with rival streaming services launching before HBO Max, that pricing may feel steep to many consumers. Disney Plus is $7 a month, and   Apple TV Plus is $5. Both are offering deals that can cut the price further or are providing opportunities for extended free periods lasting up to a year.  Apple, for example, is giving away a year's subscription to anyone who's purchased an Apple device since early September. And Disney set up a deal with  Verizon so any of the carrier's mobile subscribers get a year free.
   After that, HBO Max's first international expansions will prioritize Latin America and Europe. Latin America will get HBO Max in 2021, the company has said.
 HBO Max's US timing will put it near the end of a parade of streaming service launches. Disney Plus and Apple TV Plus launched in November. April brought Quibi, a mobile-only streaming service that features short, episodic originals. Also in April, NBCUniversal's Peacock launched a "sneak peek" at its service on Comcast. Peacock's national launch is set for July 15.
  HBO Max will be available on a wide variety of devices, including phones, tablets, the web, connected TVs and game consoles.
 But two crucial streaming devices didn't have HBO Max apps at launch. Neither Roku and Amazon Fire TV devices supported HBO Max, even though those devices represent the vast majority of streaming devices in the US.  Researcher Parks Associates estimates nearly 70% of installed streaming-TV devices in the country are either Roku or Amazon Fire TV products.
 Earlier in May, AT&T incoming CEO John Stankey warned that Amazon's Fire TV products may not have an HBO Max app. "I'm pleased to say we're going to be in virtually all app stores, or maybe one exception. It looks like we may not be in the Amazon Fire app store when all of this is said and done," he said.
  Most Apple devices that can stream video will support HBO Max -- iPhone, iPad, iPod Touch, Apple TV 4K and Apple TV HD. However, customers with second- and third-generation Apple TV models will need to stream HBO Max content from their iPhone or iPad to their TV with AirPlay.
 Google's Android operating system for phones and tablets will have an app. Android TV devices, Chromebooks, Google Chromecast and Chromecast built-in devices will support HBO Max too.
 Sony's PlayStation 4 systems will be able to download the HBO Max app via the PlayStation Store.
 Microsoft's Xbox One gaming consoles will also be able to get the HBO Max app via the Microsoft Store.
 Select Samsung internet-connected smart TVs -- models from 2016 through 2020 -- will be able to download and purchase HBO Max directly.
  In a long-anticipated feature for anyone streaming HBO Go, HBO Max has downloads for its mobile apps. Unfortunately, like most things with HBO Max, the company doesn't have a clear answer for whether there are limitations on the amount of programming you can dowload.
 "We don't have a crisp answer for you on how many downloads will be allowed but it's going to be a very comfortable number for most people," Forssell said. "The limit will probably be how much space you have in your device versus our limit, and that will liberalize over time."
 HBO Max allows you to stream to three different devices simultaneously. By comparison, Disney Plus allows four simultaneous streams, and Netflix allows between one and four depending on how much you pay each month.
 You can also create multiple profiles to keep recommendations separate for different members of the household, and it also has kid accounts that are unlocked by entering a PIN. These parental controls allow moms and dads to set the maturity levels of the programming each kids' profile can access, based ratings like PG and TV-14.
 For HBO Now members, HBO Max automatically replaces the HBO Now app through a software update.
 But there's a downside to the transition from HBO Now and HBO Go to HBO Max: HBO Max won't preserve any of your watch history if you switch from an existing HBO streaming account. So your personalized recommendations start with a clean slate, and HBO Max won't know, for example, if you've already watch every episode of Game of Thrones three times or if you're halfway through the movie Joker and want to pick back up where you left off.
  The simplest way to get HBO Max is to sign up for it directly at  hbomax.com.
  Through Apple, HBO Max will be an iOS app for iPhone and iPad, which will allow you to sign up and pay through an in-app purchase. Apple will also integrate HBO Max into its own TV app, which allows you to congregate certain streaming channels to watch them in one place.
 Through Google, HBO Max will have an app for Android phones and tablets in the Google Play store, and you'll be able to sign up inside the app with an in-app purchase. You'll also be able to stream HBO Max on Android TV, Chromebook and Google Chromecast. If you already subscribe to HBO Now through Google Play, you'll automatically be able to log in to HBO Max at launch at no additional charge, and you'll continue to be billed through Google. And YouTube TV, the streaming pay-TV service operated by Google's YouTube, will also carry HBO Max.
 People with Samsung Smart TVs that support the HBO Max app will also be able to sign up directly there.
  Things get more complicated if you already have HBO and want to know what the launch of HBO Max means for you. Some people who already have an existing subscription to HBO are able to unlock HBO Max at no extra cost,   but not everyone.
 Here's some good news for anyone who is an HBO Now subscriber through Apple or Google: You barely have to do anything to unlock HBO Max. Your HBO Now app should automatically be replaced by the HBO Max app via a software update on devices that support HBO Max. In those cases, when you open the new HBO Max app, you just need to agree to the HBO Max Terms of Use and Privacy Policy and choose to "Access All of HBO Max," and you're set, according to an  HBO Now support page.
 Some HBO Max distributors unlock an HBO Max upgrade at no extra cost for their current HBO and HBO Now customers, thanks to deals HBO Max has struck with them. You qualify for the HBO Max upgrade at no extra cost if you already pay for HBO through:
 AT&T (HBO's parent company) and DirecTV, U-verse, AT&T TV Now, AT&T TV and AT&T wireless users who can add HBO with certain unlimited plans
        The NCTC group of independent cable and broadband companies -- that includes WOW!, Atlantic Broadband, RCN, Grande Communications & Wave, MCTV and others. ( Check if your pay-TV provider is an NCTC member.)
 However, it's unclear if HBO Max will strike more deals with other pay-TV providers, whether that's traditional providers like Comcast or  Dish Network, or internet-based ones like  Amazon Prime Video Channels and  The Roku Channel.
 AT&T is also offering a complicated matrix of promos for HBO Max. Some customers get Max at no extra cost, while others qualify for a one-month, three-month or one-year free trial. Your best shot at figuring out whether you qualify is to check your account or the myAT&T app, but CNET also lists AT&T's qualification terms for its   HBO Max promos.
                                               HBO Max includes all the shows and movies on HBO, plus a selection of high-profile licensed content, like Friends, The Big Bang Theory, Rick & Morty and South Park. It'll layer   exclusive original programming on top of that. At launch, HBO Max said it'll have 10,000 hours of content.
 Originally, HBO Max planned to have 31 originals in its first year and amp that up to 50 originals in the second year. But those plans may be adjusted because of disruptions from the coronavirus.
 The simplest part of the catalog to understand is HBO itself. HBO Max includes everything on regular HBO, so that means all the theatrical movies that come and go from the channel; new episodes from all HBO series, like Westworld and Last Week Tonight With John Oliver; and the past seasons of those programs as well as the full HBO back catalog, like Game of Thrones, Silicon Valley, Veep and The Wire; plus any of the HBO original films, documentaries and specials.
 Movies are be a crucial part of the catalog. And any movie that's available to watch on the traditional HBO network will also be available to stream on HBO Max. Highlights of the HBO Max film catalog will include (but not necessarily be available at launch):
 The so-called Snyder cut of Justice League, slated to arrive on HBO Max in 2021
 The full sets of The Hobbit, The Lord of the Rings and The Matrix trilogies.
  DC films like Joker, Wonder Woman, Suicide Squad, Shazam, Aquaman and every Batman and Superman movie of the last 40 years.
 Studio Ghibli anime films that have never been released for streaming in the US before.
 Anime selections like Fullmetal Alchemist: Brotherhood and other top titles from anime-focused streaming service Crunchyroll, which is also owned by AT&T.
 Classic movies like The Wizard of Oz, Casablanca, Citizen Kane, Gone With the Wind, The Shining, Singin' in the Rain, 2001: A Space Odyssey, The Goonies, Blade Runner: The Final Cut, the Nightmare on Elm Street franchise and many others.
  On top of that, AT&T has been licensing outside hit shows to beef up HBO Max's catalog. The most most eye-popping deals have been:
 A reported $425 million deal for all episodes of '90s hit  Friends, which exited Netflix to start streaming on HBO Max at launch
 It's also secured deals for past seasons of Rick and Morty; 11 of the most recent seasons of  Doctor Who, plus another three to come; CW dramas like Pretty Little Liars, Batwoman, and Riverdale spinoff Katy Keene as well as more upcoming CW series; The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air; The West Wing; and British series like Luther and Ricky Gervais' original The Office, among many others.
 Animated satire South Park will not only contribute its full back catalog to HBO Max but also stream new episodes. The latest episodes of South Park will stream on HBO Max 24 hours after they air on Comedy Central for the next three seasons.
 And HBO Max will have exclusive new shows and movies called Max Originals. For the most part, original series on HBO Max will drop new episodes on a weekly basis.
  Love Life, the romantic comedy starring Anna Kendrick and produced by Kendrick and Paul Feig, known for blockbuster comedies like Bridesmaids
   Esme & Roy, the second season of the preschool animated series from Sesame Workshop -- June 25.
 Search Party, the third season of the comedy thriller that originally aired on TBS -- June 25.
 Adventure Time: Distant Lands-BMO, which is the first of four specials resurrecting Cartoon Network's franchise Adventure Time -- June 25.
 Expecting Amy, a three-part docuseries about comedian Amy Schumer's life on tour during her pregnancy -- July 9.
   Tig n' Seek, an animated children's series from Cartoon Network Studios about an 8-year-old and his gadget-building cat -- July 23.
 Frayed, a scripted comedy about a wealthy Londoner who goes back to her Australian hometown -- July 30.
  American Pickle, the Seth Rogen comedy that is HBO Max's first original film, about a 1920s immigrant who gets preserved in pickle brine for 100 years and meets his great-grandson (both characters portrayed by Rogen) in modern-day Brooklyn -- Aug. 6.
 Then over the course of the fall and beyond, HBO Max will add other originals, including:
 The Flight Attendant, starring and executive produced by Kaley Cuoco based on the novel by Chris Bohjalian.
  Sci-fi epic Raised by Wolves from director and executive producer Ridley Scott. The 10-episode series focuses on two androids raising human children on a mysterious planet. The show had already wrapped production in South Africa.
 HBO Max originally planned to have 31 original series in its launch year (which totals 69 series when combined with HBO content). Then in 2021, HBO Max planned to expand Max Originals to a slate of 50 shows. About half are targeted at young adults aged 16 to 34, and the rest will be divided between more adult fare and kids and family content outside the HBO brand.
  New DC titles. A  Green Lantern series that producer Greg Berlanti (a young-adult TV guru b
......
title: HBO Max通过Human Curation与Netflix竞争，而不是仅仅依赖算法
title_en: HBO Max Takes on Netflix With Human Curation Instead of Solely Relying on Algorithms

据electrek报道，特斯拉已经在北美降低了Model 3、Model S和Model X的价格。Model S和X的入门级Long Range Plus版本的价格都下调了5000美元，分别降至74990美元和79990美元，而它们更昂贵的高性能版本也下调了5000美元。Model 3在所有版本中的降价幅度较小，均为2000美元，在线入门级Standard Range Plus Model 3现在的起价为37,990美元。特斯拉最新款车型ModelY的价格没有变化。
特斯拉官方账号的一条微博证实，该公司还将降低在中国销售的Model S和Model X汽车的价格。然而，作为变化的一部分，其在中国国内生产的Model 3汽车在中国的价格不会下调。
这家电动汽车公司没有给出降价的官方原因，但路透社报道称，在新冠肺炎疫情导致汽车需求下降后，特斯拉正试图刺激销售。据报道，与去年相比，4月份的汽车零售额下降了一半，因为人们遵守了留在家里的命令，减少了非必要的旅行。通用汽车(GM)、福特(Ford)和菲亚特克莱斯勒(Fiat Chrysler)等其他汽车制造商提供0%的融资和延期付款选择。
note_en:
Tesla has reduced the price of the Model 3, Model S, and Model X in North America,   Electrek reports. The Model S and X both saw $5,000 price cuts to $74,990 and $79,990 respectively for their entry-level Long Range Plus versions, while their more expensive Performance versions have also been cut by $5k. The Model 3 has received a smaller $2,000 price cut across all versions, with the online entry-level Standard Range Plus Model 3 now starting at $37,990. Pricing for the Model Y, Tesla’s newest car, is unchanged.
 A  Weibo post from Tesla’s official account confirmed that the company will also reduce the prices of the Model S and Model X cars sold in China. However, the Chinese price of its Model 3 cars, which it produces domestically in the country, will not be cut as part of the changes.
 The electric car company did not give an official reason for the price reductions, but   Reuters reports that Tesla is trying to stimulate sales after a decline in vehicle demand caused by the COVID-19 pandemic. Auto retail sales reportedly halved in April compared to the previous year, as people obeyed stay at home orders and cut down on non-essential travel. Other automakers such as GM, Ford, and Fiat Chrysler are offering 0 percent financing and deferred payment options.
title: 特斯拉将汽车价格下调高达5000美元
title_en: Tesla cuts car prices by up to $5k

跳转到导航跳跃以搜索天花或接种是最早使用从患者或最近进行了天花接种的个人身上提取的材料来为个人接种天花(天花)的方法，希望能产生轻微的、但具有保护性的感染。这一过程最常见的方法是将脓疱中的天花结痂或液体粉末插入/摩擦到皮肤上形成的表面划痕中。患者会出现与自然发生的天花相同的脓疱，通常产生的疾病没有自然获得的天花那么严重。最终，大约两到四周后，这些症状会消退，表明成功康复和免疫。这种方法首先在中国和中东使用，然后在17世纪20年代面对一些反对意见被引入英国和北美。这种方法今天已经不再使用了。取而代之的是天花疫苗，一种更安全的替代品。这反过来又导致了许多现在可用的针对其他疾病的疫苗的开发。
用来描述预防天花的术语可能会引起混淆。在18世纪的医学术语中，接种指的是接种天花。混乱是由作家通过误译或曲解而互换变种和疫苗造成的。术语“变种”仅指接种天花病毒，不能与接种疫苗互换。后者在1800年爱德华·詹纳(Edward Jenner)推出牛痘疫苗后不久首次使用，牛痘是一种与天花截然不同的动物疾病。从19世纪开始，人们开始使用“变种”一词，以避免与接种疫苗混淆。大多数现代作家倾向于将天花接种称为自始至终的变种，而不考虑时间顺序，就像这里使用的那样。1891年，路易斯·巴斯德(Louis Pasteur)向詹纳致敬，扩大了疫苗/疫苗接种一词的范围，以指人工诱导对任何传染病的免疫力，这引起了进一步的混乱。接种是注射的同义词，与疫苗或其他生物制药的使用有关，但在实验室工作中也有其他含义。
中国人实践着最古老的变奏用法，可以追溯到15世纪。他们实施了一种鼻腔注气的方法，通过将粉状天花物质(通常是结痂)吹到鼻孔上来实施。在整个十六、十七世纪，中国都有各种注气技术的记载。[1]：根据这些文件，选择轻度天花病例作为捐赠者，以防止严重的攻击。这项技术使用的是留了一段时间晾干的结痂。新鲜的结痂更有可能导致全面感染。三四个结痂被磨成粉末或与一粒麝香混合在一起，然后用棉花捆扎起来。被感染的物质随后被装进一根管子，并鼓起病人的鼻孔。据信，中国人已经把变奏仪式仪式化了。手术过程中使用的吹管是银制的。男孩子用右鼻孔，女孩子用左鼻孔。[2]：对45例不同的病例进行了治疗，就好像他们与自然感染该病的人一样具有传染性。这些患者随后被隔离，直到皮疹消失。1700年，伦敦皇家学会(Royal Society)收到了两份关于中国实践的报告；一份是马丁·李斯特(Martin Lister)博士的报告，他收到了东印度公司驻华员工的报告；另一份是克莱普顿·哈弗斯(Clopton Havers)医生的报告。但没有采取任何行动。[3]。
印度被认为是杂色艺术传播到欧洲，特别是奥斯曼帝国和威尔士的另一个可能的来源地。有两个帐户从18世纪的巡回婆罗门正在进行的疫苗接种：奥利弗库尔特在1731年写到，它已第一次执行由邓自愿约150年前约150年前约150年(丹万塔里是印度教药神的名字，在医生中的一个常见的名字)，和约翰Zephaniah Holwell在1768年写到它是被实践了数百年之久。(译者注：Dhanvantari是印度教药神的名字，也是医生中的常用名)，而John Zephaniah Holwell在1768年写道，它已经实行了数百年。(Dhanvantari是印度教药神的名字，也是医生中的一个常见名字)。这些说法的某些方面受到了质疑，但不管怎样，到了1768年，孟加拉就开始接种疫苗了。[4]。
类似的方法在中东和非洲也可以看到。在十八世纪末和十九世纪初，苏丹描述了两种类似的方法。这两种做法都由来已久，都源于阿拉伯的做法。Tishteree el Jidderi(#34；购买天花)是在苏丹中部塞纳尔的妇女中看到的一种做法。[1]61无保护措施的孩子的母亲会去新感染的孩子的家里，在生病的孩子的胳膊上绑一块棉布。然后她会和孩子的母亲讨价还价
英国杂色运动的主要支持者是萨顿家族，这是一个医生家族，他们将彻底改变这一做法。族长罗伯特·萨顿(Robert Sutton)是萨福克的一名外科医生。1757年，他的一个儿子的手术失败了。[5]：20他寻求一种新的方法，使程序尽可能变得温和。到1762年，他开始宣传一种新的接种天花疫苗的方法。萨顿对他的方法保密，只与他的三个儿子分享。这种新方法背后的神秘性和有效性有助于促进他们的业务，很快就变得非常成功。他们建立了一个杂货店和诊所网络，并向其他杂货店提供特许经营权，以分享利润，条件是这个秘密不会泄露。到1770年，萨顿夫妇已经接待了30多万名满意的顾客。[1][1]萨顿家族的长子丹尼尔最终在他1796年出版的《接种器》一书中透露了家庭秘密。[5]：22他们方法的成功之处在于划痕浅，仔细选择仅受轻微影响的捐赠者，没有出血或过度排泄。虽然萨顿家族的名气在这一发现后逐渐褪色，但这家人给人的持久印象将世世代代保留下来。
其他著名的英国变种人包括托马斯·丁斯代尔(Thomas Dimsdale)，他在1769年和1781年发表了对他的方法的描述；威廉·伍德维尔(William Woodville)，1791年被任命为伦敦天花和接种医院院长，他在1796年出版了一部杂种史；约翰·海加思(John Haygarth)，他在1783年发表了一项雄心勃勃的消灭天花的计划。
约翰·威廉姆森(John Williamson，绰号Johnnie Notions更广为人知)是一位自学成才的医生[11]：571名来自苏格兰设得兰的医生，他在18世纪末独立开发了一种天花疫苗，并成功地治疗了设得兰的数千名患者。尽管只受过基础教育，没有正规的医学背景，[12]：208他设计的治疗的有效性产生了极高的成功率，导致约3000人接种了疫苗，挽救了许多人的生命，这对当时设得兰人口的人口结构产生了重大影响。[13]402据说他没有失去一个病人。[11]：571。
观念首先会收集天花脓液。然后，他会用泥炭烟(据信可以降低病毒的毒力)，[13]：401将其晾干，然后用樟脑[11]：571(具有抗菌特性，防止物质分解)将其埋在地下。[13]*401口述历史表明，这件事在埋葬前散布在玻璃片之间。[14]在给患者使用之前，它将保持这种状态长达7或8年，以降低其毒性。[11]571使用刀子(意念自己制造的)，他会在病人的手臂上切开皮内(不抽血)，插入少量的物质，立即用病人的皮肤覆盖伤口，然后用卷心菜叶做石膏。[11]571与同时代的江湖医生不同，这些观念不会规定任何特定的休息条件(例如，热治疗--在火前加热病人，用毯子盖住他们，不让他们呼吸新鲜空气)，[13]：398在感染和康复期间，他也不会给他们服用任何其他药物。(#*_)。[11]：571。
变分概念与萨顿方法有很大的相似之处。观念是如何意识到这种变异方法的还不清楚-它可能是通过书面陈述，或者通过与其他了解这种技术的人进行讨论，比如另一名医生或神职人员。[13]：401。
1738年，Variolation被添加到第二版的Chambers&#39；Cyclopædia中，它在当时是文学类知识的权威。1754年晚些时候，Variolation获得了皇家医师学院的批准。[2]*47所有这些都使英格兰成为国际多样性中心，吸引了来自世界各地的游客探索这一新的预防方法。这个国家也像磁铁一样吸引着那些试图将多样化的好处介绍给自己国家的人。这方面的一个显著例子是将杂色引入俄罗斯。著名的银行家、政治家和医生托马斯·丁斯代尔应邀访问圣彼得堡，为凯瑟琳大帝增光添彩。1769年，他对凯瑟琳、她14岁的儿子保罗大公，以及140多名杰出的宫廷成员进行了抨击。结果是成功的。丁斯代尔被封为俄罗斯帝国的男爵，授予10000英镑，2000英镑的开支和500英镑的年金。陪同他的儿子也得到了奖励。万一丁斯代尔的杂耍活动以糟糕的结局收场，凯瑟琳安排了一场接力赛马活动。
在全国范围内进行系统接种，隔离患者，净化可能受污染的病原体，对特定地区负责的监督检查员，对穷人遵守隔离规则的奖励，对违反这些规则的罚款，在港口检查船只，以及每周日祈祷。
由于后勤原因和各种疾病传播天花的风险，它在当时的实施是不切实际的。然而，经过适当的修改，例如用疫苗接种代替疫苗接种，它与世界卫生组织在根除天花运动中采取的战略非常相似。
美洲关于变种的记录可以追溯到1706年的波士顿，清教徒牧师科顿·马瑟(Cotton Mather)从他的西非奴隶奥尼西姆斯(Onesimus)那里学到了这种技术。[2][2]45对此事的进一步研究向马瑟透露，其他几个奴隶也被混杂在一起。1714年，他偶然在“哲学学报”上看到蒂莫尼的文章，在文章中他描述了在土耳其发现的变种方法。1721年波士顿爆发天花时，马瑟得以实施这一新方法，尽管威廉·道格拉斯等人强烈反对这一想法。
反对多种多样的主要论点是基于宗教原因。由于宗教在18世纪的波士顿从未远离生活的任何方面，一些人想知道这种新方法如何与宗教教义相吻合。最简单的辩论认为，杂耍是不敬神的，因为圣经中没有特别提到它。一些人还认为，接种疫苗是对上帝与生俱来的权利的直接冒犯，上帝有权决定谁将死亡，以及死亡将如何以及何时发生。一些人认为，天花的爆发是对那些感染了这种疾病的人的罪过的当之无愧的惩罚。那些经验主义的人认为，使用这种致命疾病的产品来预防这种疾病的想法是对逻辑的侮辱。[1]：142。
尽管有这些持续不断的争论，马瑟也获得了几个支持者。这群追随者中有外科医生扎布迪尔·博伊尔斯顿(Zabdiel Boylston)，他敦促马瑟进一步推广这一程序。在马瑟的支持下，博伊尔斯顿继续成功地对300名患者进行了多样化治疗，其中只有6名患者死亡。相比之下，在同一时期，6000名感染天花的人中有1000人自然死亡。博伊尔斯顿于1724年前往伦敦。他在那里发表了他的研究成果，并于1726年当选为皇家学会会员。[1]：144。
从波士顿开始，这种做法传遍了整个殖民地。1775年，乔治·华盛顿下令对大陆陆军进行分门别类。到美国独立战争结束时，五花八门在美国的大城市和大城镇得到了广泛的接受。[2]：47。
杂交化的成功导致许多人，包括医学专业人士，忽视了它的缺点。实行杂花疗法的基础是，它可以终生预防天花，而且比自然感染更不可能致死。然而，在某些情况下，天然天花或天花未能保护自己免受第二次攻击。这些病例是免疫记忆丧失的结果，而其他病例可能被误诊了(专家经常将天花与水痘混淆)。多种多样还需要一定程度的技能和对细节的关注，而这是一些医生所缺乏的。许多内科医生没有注意到局部发红和流出，以确保已经采取了各种措施，导致治疗不足。然而，正是它给其他人带来的巨大风险导致了这种做法的结束。多种多样的受试者在接种后不久传播的侧枝天花病例开始超过手术带来的好处。
从17世纪60年代开始，许多人，包括约翰·菲斯特、彼得·皮莱特、本杰明·杰斯特，特别是爱德华·詹纳，都对使用牛痘(一种动物传染病)中的物质来预防天花感兴趣。[17][18]1796年，詹纳给詹姆斯·菲普斯接种了疫苗，1798年又进行了更多的疫苗接种，并第一个发表了证据表明牛痘可以预防天花，比接种疫苗更安全，而且他的疫苗可以通过手臂到手臂的转移来维持。[19]随着天花疫苗的广泛使用和其益处的增加，各种疫苗的使用很快就开始减少。从1805年的俄罗斯开始，各个国家都将变种定为非法。[9]：246
品种繁多是发现疫苗的天然先驱。两者的主要区别是，在接种疫苗时，使用了来自动物疾病牛痘的材料，但特别是对那些接种疫苗的人来说是安全的，不会传染给他们的接触者。提供疫苗接种。

Jump to navigation  Jump to search  Variolation or  inoculation was the method first used to  immunize an individual against  smallpox ( Variola) with material taken from a patient or a recently variolated individual, in the hope that a mild, but protective, infection would result. The procedure was most commonly carried out by inserting/rubbing powdered smallpox scabs or fluid from pustules into superficial scratches made in the skin. The patient would develop pustules identical to those caused by naturally occurring smallpox, usually producing a less severe disease than naturally acquired smallpox. Eventually, after about two to four weeks, these symptoms would subside, indicating successful recovery and immunity. The method was first used in China and the Middle East before it was introduced into England and North America in the 1720s in the face of some opposition. The method is no longer used today. It was replaced by  smallpox vaccine, a safer alternative. This in turn led to the development of the many  vaccines now available against other diseases.
   The terminology used to describe the prevention of smallpox can cause confusion. In 18th-century medical terminology,   inoculation refers to  smallpox inoculation. Confusion is caused by writers who interchange  variolation and   vaccination through either mistranslation or misinterpretation. The term  variolation refers solely to inoculation with smallpox virus and is not interchangeable with  vaccination. The latter term was first used in 1800 soon after  Edward Jenner introduced smallpox vaccine derived from  cowpox, an animal disease distinct from smallpox. The term  variolation was then used from the 19th century to avoid confusion with vaccination. Most modern writers tend to refer to smallpox inoculation as variolation throughout without regard for chronology, as is used here. Further confusion was caused when, in 1891,  Louis Pasteur honoured Jenner by widening the terms  vaccine/vaccination to refer to the  artificial induction of immunity against any  infectious disease.  Inoculation is used synonymously with  injection in connection with the use of vaccines or other biopharmaceuticals, but has other meanings in e.g. laboratory work.
   The Chinese practiced the oldest documented use of variolation, dating back to the fifteenth century. They implemented a method of "nasal  insufflation" administered by blowing powdered smallpox material, usually scabs, up the nostrils. Various insufflation techniques have been recorded throughout the sixteenth and seventeenth centuries within China.  [1] : 60 According to such documentation, mild smallpox cases were selected as donors in order to prevent serious attack. The technique used scabs that had been left to dry out for some time. Fresh scabs were more likely to lead to a full-blown infection. Three or four scabs were ground into powder or mixed with a grain of musk and bound in cotton. Infected material was then packed into a pipe and puffed up the patient's nostril. The practice of variolation is believed to have been  ritualized by the Chinese. The blowpipe used during the procedure was made of silver. The right nostril was used for boys and the left for girls.  [2] : 45 Variolated cases were treated as if they were as infectious as those who had acquired the disease naturally. These patients were subsequently kept apart from others until the rash had cleared. Two reports on the Chinese practice were received by the  Royal Society in London in 1700; one by Dr.  Martin Lister who received a report by an employee of the  East India Company stationed in China, and another by the physician  Clopton Havers. But no action was taken.  [3]
  India has been suggested as another possible origin  [4] for the spread of variolation into Europe, particularly the Ottoman Empire and Wales. There are two accounts from the 18th century of inoculations being performed by itinerant Brahmins: Oliver Coult in 1731 wrote that it had been "first performed by Dununtary a physician of Champanagar" about 150 years before that time ( Dhanvantari being the name of the Hindu God of medicine, and a common name among physicians), and  John Zephaniah Holwell in 1768 wrote of its being practiced for many hundreds of years. Aspects of these accounts have been called into question, but at any rate, by 1768 inoculation was being practiced in Bengal.  [4]
  Similar methods were seen through the Middle East and Africa. Two similar methods were described in  Sudan during the late eighteenth and early nineteenth centuries. Both had been long established and stemmed from Arabic practices.  Tishteree el Jidderi ("buying the smallpox") was a practice seen within the women of  Sennar in Central Sudan.  [1] : 61 A mother of an unprotected child would visit the house of a newly infected child and tie a cotton cloth around the ailing child's arm. She would then  haggle with the child's mother over the cost of each pustule. When a bargain was struck, the woman would return home and tie the cloth around her own child's arm. Variations of this practice included bringing gifts to the donor. The second method was known as  Dak el Jedri ("hitting the smallpox"),  [1] : 61 a method similar to that used in  Turkey and eventually transported into England. Fluid was collected from a smallpox pustule and rubbed into a cut made into the patient's skin. This practice spread more widely through Africa. It may have also traveled with merchants and pilgrims along the middle-eastern caravan routes into Turkey and Greece.  [5] : 15
   Although variolation had become common practice in China and much of Africa by the seventeenth century, Western European medicine still saw the practice as being nothing more than  folklore. For instance, letters from physicians in Wales published in 1722 indicated its local use from as early as 1600 near the Welsh port of  Haverfordwest.  [6] It would not be until Italian physician Dr.  Emmanuel Timoni of  Constantinople promoted the practice that variolation began its spread through Western Europe. After coming across the practice in Constantinople, Timoni wrote a letter describing the method in detail which was later published in the  Philosophical Transactions in early 1714.  [7] : 77 His account would become the first medical account of variolation to appear in Europe. Although the article did not gain widespread notoriety, it caught the attention of two important figures in the variolation movement, Bostonian preacher  Cotton Mather and the wife of the British Ambassador to the  Ottoman Empire,  Lady Mary Wortley Montagu.
  No stranger to smallpox,  Lady Mary had lost her brother to the devastating disease. Soon afterwards, she also contracted smallpox. Although she survived, she was left with severe facial scarring. While in Turkey she came across the process of variolation as it was introduced and published in Constantinople by two doctors, Emmanuel Timoni and Iacob Pylarino.   [8]. She first mentioned variolation in the famous letter to her friend, Sarah Chiswell, in April 1717.  [9] : 55 in which she enthusiastically recounted the process, which in Constantinople was most commonly administered by experienced elderly women. In 1718, she had the procedure conducted on her five-year-old son, Edward Montagu. The procedure was supervised by the embassy doctor  Charles Maitland. On her return to England, she had her four-year-old daughter variolated in the presence of physicians of the Royal Court in 1721.  [1] : 90 Both variolations proved successful. Later on that year Maitland conducted an experimental variolation of six prisoners in  Newgate Prison in London. In the experiment, six condemned prisoners were variolated and later exposed to smallpox with the promise of freedom if they survived.  [2] : 45 The experiment was a success, and soon variolation was drawing attention from the royal family, who helped promote the procedure throughout England. However, variolation caused the death of  Prince Octavius of Great Britain, eighth son and thirteenth child of  King George III in 1783.  [10]
  Despite opposition, variolation established itself as a mainstream medical treatment in England. Part of its success was founded on statistical observation, which confirmed that variolation was a safer alternative to contracting smallpox naturally, strengthened by the assumption that it protected against the disease for life. The major faults of variolation lay in its simplicity. Doctors sought to monopolize the simple treatment by convincing the public that the procedure could only be done by a trained professional. The procedure was now preceded by a severe  bloodletting, in which the patient was bled, often to faintness, in order to 'purify' the blood and prevent fever. Doctors also began to favour deep incisions, which also discouraged amateurs.  [5] : 18
  The main proponents of the English variolation movement were the Suttons, a family of physicians who would revolutionize the practice. The patriarch, Robert Sutton, was a surgeon from  Suffolk. In 1757 the procedure failed on one of his sons.  [5] : 20 He sought a new method in which the procedure would become as mild as possible. By 1762 he began advertising "A New Method of Inoculating for Small-Pox." Sutton kept his method a secret and shared it only with his three sons. The mystique and effectiveness behind this new method helped to promote their business, which soon became very successful. They established a network of variolation houses and clinics and offered franchises to other variolators for a share of the profits, on condition that the secret would not be revealed. By 1770, the Suttons had treated over 300,000 satisfied customers.  [1] : 94 Daniel, the eldest of the Sutton sons, eventually revealed the family secret in his book  The Inoculator, published in 1796.  [5] : 22 The success of their method lay in a shallow scratch, careful selection of only mildly affected donors, and no bleeding or extreme purging. Although the renown of the Suttons gradually faded after this revelation, the family's lasting impression would remain for generations.
  Other prominent English variolators included  Thomas Dimsdale, who published accounts of his method in 1769 and 1781;  William Woodville, appointed Director of the London Smallpox and Inoculation Hospital in 1791, who published a history of variolation in 1796; and  John Haygarth, who published an ambitious plan to  exterminate smallpox in 1783.
   John Williamson, more commonly known by the nickname Johnnie Notions, was a self-taught  [11] : 571 physician from  Shetland,  Scotland, who independently developed and successfully administered a variolation for smallpox to thousands of patients in Shetland during the late 18th century. Despite having only an elementary education and no formal medical background,  [12] : 208 the effectiveness of the treatment he devised yielded an extremely high success rate, resulting in the immunisation of approximately 3,000 people and the saving of many lives, which had a significant effect on the demographics of the Shetland population at the time.  [13] : 402 He is reputed to have not lost a single patient.  [11] : 571
 Notions would first collect small pox  pus. He would then dry it using  peat  smoke (which was believed to lessen the virus's virulence),  [13] : 401 and bury it in the ground with  camphor  [11] : 571 (which has anti-bacterial properties, preventing the matter from decomposing).  [13] : 401 Oral history indicates the matter was spread between sheets of  glass before burial.  [14] It would be kept in this state for up to 7 or 8 years to reduce its  virulence before being administered to a patient.  [11] : 571 By using a knife (which Notions made himself) he would incise into the patient's arm  intradermally (without drawing blood), insert a small amount of the matter, and immediately cover the incision with the patient's skin, before using a  cabbage leaf as a plaster.  [11] : 571 In contrast to contemporaneous  quack doctors, Notions would not stipulate any particular resting conditions (such as "hot-treatment" – heating the ill patient in front of a fire, covering them with blankets and allowing them no fresh-air),  [13] : 398 nor would he administer any other medicines during the period of infection and recovery.  [11] : 571
 Notions' variolation bears a strong similarity to the Suttonian method. How Notions became aware of this method of variolation is unclear – it may have been through written account, or through discussion with someone else aware of the technique, such as another physician or a member of the clergy.  [13] : 401
  In 1738, variolation was added to the second edition of   Chambers' Cyclopædia, which in its time was an authority of knowledge for the literary class. Later in 1754, variolation received the sanction of the  Royal College of Physicians.  [2] : 47 All of this made England the international center of variolation, attracting visitors from all over the world to explore this "new" method of prevention. The nation also acted as a magnet for those who sought to introduce the benefits of variolation to their own countries. A remarkable example of this is the introduction of variolation into Russia.  Thomas Dimsdale, a prominent banker, politician, and physician, was invited to visit St Petersburg to variolate  Catherine the Great. In 1769, he variolated  Catherine, her 14-year-old son  Grand Duke Paul, and over 140 prominent members of the Court. The results were successful. Dimsdale was created a  baron of the  Russian Empire, awarded  £10,000, with £2000 for expenses and an annuity of £500. His son, who accompanied him, was also rewarded. In case Dimsdale's variolations had ended badly, Catherine had arranged a relay of horses to carry them safely out of the country.  [15]  [16]
 France was the last European country to embrace variolation. It was not until an outbreak of smallpox in Paris in 1752 nearly killed the heir to the French throne that the public embraced the practice after seeing the prince variolated.  [9] : 102 Similarly in Japan, Chinese merchant Li Jen-Shan proposed the method of traditional Chinese intranasal variolation after a severe smallpox outbreak in  Nagasaki in 1744. This led Japanese physician  Ogata Shunsaku  [ ja] to variolate children using a human smallpox vaccination method during an outbreak in  Chikuzen Province from 1789 to 1790.  [1] : 95 There were no deaths among the children, and they all appeared to be protected.
 By the end of the eighteenth century, variolation had gained widespread global respect and was thought to be one of the greatest medical successes of its time. It had become the subject of serious medical study, leading physicians like  John Haygarth from  Chester, England, to explore its application on a larger scale. In 1793 he published  A Sketch of a Plan to Exterminate the Small-Pox from Great Britain. This relied on rules summarised by Donald Hopkins;  [15] : 77
 Systematic inoculation throughout the country, isolation of patients, decontamination of potentially contaminated fomites, supervised inspectors responsible for specific districts, rewards for observation of rules for isolation by poor persons, fines for transgression of those rules, inspection of vessels at ports, and prayers every Sunday.
 Its implementation at the time was impractical for logistical reasons and the risk that variolation would spread smallpox. However, with suitable modifications, such as the substitution of vaccination for variolation, it was remarkably similar to the strategy adopted during the  World Health Organization's smallpox eradication campaign.
  Documentation of variolation in the Americas may be traced back to 1706 in  Boston, where  Puritan minister  Cotton Mather learned of the technique from his West African slave  Onesimus.  [2] : 45 Further research into the matter revealed to Mather that several other slaves had too been variolated. In 1714, he came across Timoni's article in  Philosophical Transactions, in which he described methods of variolation found in Turkey. Mather was able to implement this new method in 1721 when Boston suffered a smallpox outbreak,  [7] although others such as  William Douglass strongly opposed the idea.
 The main arguments against variolation were on religious grounds. Because religion was never far from any aspect of life in eighteenth-century Boston, several wondered how this new method would coincide with religious teachings. The simplest debate argued that variolation was ungodly because it was not mentioned specifically in the Bible. Inoculation was also viewed by some as a direct affront to God's innate right to determine who was to die, and how and when death would occur. Several believed smallpox outbreaks were well-merited punishments for the sins of those who contracted the disease. Those who were empirically minded saw the notion of using the products of such a deadly disease to prevent said disease as being an insult to logic.  [1] : 142
 Despite these persistent arguments, Mather also gained several supporters. Among this group of followers was surgeon  Zabdiel Boylston, who urged Mather to further promote the procedure. With the support of Mather, Boylston went on to successfully variolate 300 patients, with only six of them dying. By contrast, 1,000 of the 6,000 people who acquired smallpox naturally died during the same period. Boylston traveled to London in 1724. There he published his results and was elected to the Royal Society in 1726.  [1] : 144
 From Boston, the practice spread throughout the colonies. In 1775,  George Washington ordered that the  Continental Army be variolated. By the end of the  American Revolutionary War, variolation had gained widespread acceptance in the larger cities and towns of the United States.  [2] : 47
  The success of variolation led many, including medical professionals, to overlook its drawbacks. Variolation was practiced on the basis that it protected against smallpox for life, and was far less likely to kill than natural infection. In some cases however, natural smallpox or variolation failed to protect from a second attack. These cases were a result of a lapse of immune "memory", while others may have been misdiagnosed (experts often confused smallpox with  chickenpox). Variolation also required a level of skill and attention to detail which some physicians lacked. Many physicians failed to take note of local redness and discharge to assure the variolation had taken, resulting in inadequate treatment. However, it was its great risk to others that led to the end of the practice. The collateral smallpox cases spread by variolated subjects shortly after variolation began to outweigh the benefits of the procedure.
 From the 1760s, a number of individuals, including  John Fewster,  Peter Plett,  Benjamin Jesty, and particularly  Edward Jenner, were interested in the use of material from  cowpox, an animal infection, to protect against smallpox.  [17]  [18] In 1796, Jenner vaccinated  James Phipps, did more vaccinations in 1798, and was the first to publish evidence that cowpox protected against smallpox, was safer than variolation, and that his vaccine could be maintained by arm-to-arm transfer.  [19] The use of variolation soon began to decline as the  smallpox vaccine became widely used and its benefits appreciated. Various countries made variolation illegal, starting with Russia in 1805.  [9] : 246
 Variolation served as a natural precursor to the discovery of vaccination. The major differences between the two were that in vaccination, material from cowpox, an animal disease, was used, but particularly that it was safe to those vaccinated and was not transmitted to their contacts. Vaccination offered
......

note:
特朗普又一次加倍下注。在关于邮寄选票的推文中增加了事实核查警告标签后，特朗普再次在平台上谴责这一行为。总统在很大程度上让他自由发挥了这一点，这可能是他迄今为止对这项服务发表的最强烈的言论，他建议社交媒体服务必须受到监管，否则就必须关闭。共和党人长期以来一直认为社交媒体网站怀有反保守派的偏见。
他在推特上写道：“共和党人觉得社交媒体平台完全压制了保守派的声音。”“在我们允许这种情况发生之前，我们将对它们进行强有力的监管，或者关闭它们。我们看到了他们在2016年试图做的事情，但失败了。“。
最后一点似乎指的是Twitter和Facebook等平台在2016年大选中扮演的角色。特朗普随后重申了早先关于邮寄选票的说法，指责在大流行的情况下推动便捷投票是一种“对所有人都免费的作弊、伪造和盗窃”。
正是这些说法让他一开始就获得了Twitter的事实核查标签。然而，截至撰写本文时，美国东部时间今天早上7点多发出的这条新推文还没有添加这样的标签。在漫长的假期周末之后，特朗普在他最喜欢的社交媒体平台上度过了忙碌的几天。昨晚，他指责这项服务“扼杀了言论自由”，尽管推特长期以来不愿删除推文，也不愿因特朗普违反TOS而禁止他。
今天早上，总统在推特上再次将一位有线新闻早间节目主持人与关于他已故妻子主持人的旧阴谋论联系在一起，并宣布“奥巴马门”比水门事件更糟糕。
note_en:
Once again, Trump has doubled down. Following the addition of a fact-checking warning label added to his tweet about mail-in ballots, Trump took to the platform yet again to denounce it. In what may be his strongest words to date against a service that has largely given him free rein to this point, the President suggested that social media services would have to be regulated or shut down. Republicans have long held that social media sites harbor an anti-conservative bias.
 “Republicans feel that Social Media Platforms totally silence conservatives voices,” he  tweeted. “We will strongly regulate, or close them down, before we can ever allow this to happen. We saw what they attempted to do, and failed, in 2016.”
  That last bit appears to be a reference to the role platforms like Twitter and Facebook played in the 2016 election. Trump then went on to reassert earlier claims about mail-in ballots, accusing a push for easy access to voting amid a pandemic of being a “free for all on cheating, forgery and the theft.”
 It was precisely those claims that earned him a Twitter fact-checking label in the first place. As of this writing, however, no such label has been added to the new tweet sent a little after 7AM ET this morning. It’s been a busy couple of days for Trump on his favored social media platform, following the long holiday weekend. Last night he accused the service of “stifling free speech,” in spite of Twitter’s long-standing reluctance to either delete tweets or ban Trump over perceived TOS violations.
 This morning the President took to Twitter to once again tie a cable news morning host to an old conspiracy theory about his late-wife host and declare “Obamagate” worse than Watergate.
title: 推特事实核查后，特朗普威胁要规范或关闭社交媒体
title_en: After Twitter fact-check, Trump threatens to regulate or close down social media

note:
纽约州和新泽西州的新冠肺炎病毒一直很严重，这两个州的人均病例达到全国最高。然而，仅次于人均的是美洲原住民保留地，新冠肺炎对纳瓦霍民族和霍皮人保留地社区的打击尤其严重。
截至5月25日，自3月中旬以来，纳瓦霍民族和霍皮人保留地报告的新冠肺炎死亡病例已近5千例，死亡人数达157人。人均发病率超过2500/10万人，是美国发病率最高的国家之一。
使病毒变得更加严重的是，美洲原住民没有得到与美国其他州相同水平的援助。联邦援助几乎不存在，即使保留地内的重要企业已经关闭。自4月底以来，新冠肺炎关于保留的数据也被排除在国家的联邦数据收集之外。
乔克托民族(Choctaw Nation)和纳瓦霍民族(Navajo Nation)被迫求助于GoFundMe运动，以获得货币援助，捐款来自世界各地的普通公民。
note_en:
The COVID-19 virus in  New York and New Jersey has been severe, with cases per capita reaching the highest in the country in these two states. However, just below in cases per capita are Native American reservations, where COVID-19 is hitting the Navajo Nation and Hopi Reservation communities particularly hard.
 As of May 25, there have been  nearly 5,000 reported cases of COVID-19 in the Navajo Nation and Hopi Reservation land since the middle of March, with the death toll reaching 157. That’s a per capita case rate of over 2,500 per 100,000 people – one of the highest in the U.S.
 What’s making the virus even more severe is that Native Americans aren’t receiving the same levels of assistance as other states in the country. Federal aid is almost non-existent, even as vital businesses within reservations are closed. Since the end of April, COVID-19 data on reservations has also been left out of the country’s federal data collection.
 Choctaw Nation and Navajo Nation have been forced to resort to  GoFundMe campaigns for monetary assistance, and donations have come from private citizens across the world.
title: 纳瓦霍人和其他美洲原住民保留地被新冠肺炎重创
title_en: Navajo Nation and Other Native American Reservations Hit Hard by Covid-19

周二晚上，密苏里州共和党参议员乔希·霍利(Josh Hawley)在Twitter上回应特朗普对Twitter举动的抱怨时，也发出了类似的威胁。
title: 特朗普威胁要“关闭”社交媒体平台
title_en: Trump threatens to 'close' down social media platforms

note:
经过30年的开创性研究，英国气象局证实，气候上的人类指纹现在是毋庸置疑的，并将在接下来的几十年里变得越来越明显。
自20世纪90年代以来，全球气温上升了0.5度，北冰洋海冰缩小了近200万平方公里，海平面上升了约10厘米，大气中的二氧化碳增加了百万分之60(17%)，这是为英国气象局哈德利气候科学和服务中心成立30周年而专门编制的数据。
这些数据突显了年轻一代是如何在千禧年来前所未有的气候中长大的。未来的预测表明，到本世纪中叶，一个60岁的英国人可能会生活在比他们出生时高1.2摄氏度的气候中。
哈德利中心(Hadley Centre)的科学家们表示，关于化石燃料破坏的早期理论已经被随后的事实证明。自1990年开放以来，哈德利中心一直站在全球气候监测、研究和建模的前线。
“现在的气候与30年前完全不同。这完全超出了自然变异的可能性范围，“该中心气候归属科学教授兼专家彼得·斯托特说。
他说，在哈德利中心的早期预测中，科学家们预测，1990年至2020年间，由于石油、天然气和煤炭的排放，英国将变暖0.5摄氏度：“我们说得很对。”
他说，随着新的高温记录随着频率的增加而被打破，全球气温现在高于气象局自1850年以来的测量结果中的任何水平，或者是通过数千年前的树木年轮间接计算出来的。大气中的二氧化碳浓度也比百万年前的冰芯中看到的任何东西都要高。“我们看到了前所未有的气候，”斯托特说。“人类指纹无处不在。”
这种影响在1990年没有那么明显，当时该中心与联合国政府间气候变化专门委员会(UN IPCC On Climate Change)的第一份报告一起开放-两者都由今年早些时候去世的英国科学家约翰·霍顿(John Houghton)负责监督。
在就职典礼上，当时的首相玛格丽特·撒切尔(Margaret Thatcher)表示，英国需要一个世界领先的气候中心来评估温室气体排放的“严重后果”。“它所预测的将影响我们的日常生活。世界各地的政府和国际组织将不得不振作起来，关注并做出回应，“她说。
在牛津大学学习科学的撒切尔夫人几乎不需要令人信服，但她必须克服一个持怀疑态度的内阁。几十年来，大气物理学家一直在警告石油公司和政策制定者化石燃料的危险，但对于更广泛的公众来说，“温室效应”仍然是一个相对新鲜的担忧。没有人感觉到变化。与工业化前的水平相比，世界已经变暖了大约0.5度，但这已经低到足以在自然变化范围内。
很少有人知道短期“天气”和“气候”之间的区别，后者通常是以长得多的30年时间来测量的。
当时，人们对气象学家预测变化的能力也有相当大的怀疑，即使是在每天的基础上也是如此。BBC天气预报员迈克尔·菲什(Michael Fish)臭名昭著地向观众保证，就在几个小时后，几代人以来最猛烈的风暴袭击英国，人们对飓风的恐惧是没有根据的，这让人记忆犹新。
从一开始就被派往哈德利中心的科学研究员詹姆斯·墨菲(James Murphy)回忆道：“这很乏味。”但头十年的气氛是非常兴奋的，因为这是一个在一个不断增长的领域进行大量开创性研究的机会。
随着接受度和重要性的提高，怀疑主义也在增加。批评人士通常由石油公司资助，他们反对气候科学，因为他们不喜欢它的政治和经济影响。在京都(1997年)和哥本哈根(2009年)气候峰会之前，化石燃料公司资助了错误的信息运动，以质疑气候模型。
哈德利中心仍然专注于科学。研究人员扩展和完善了他们的模型，纳入了关于生物碳循环的新知识，以及化石燃料不仅对温度，而且对北极冰、海平面上升、洪水、风暴、干旱和其他大气现象的可能影响。
超级计算机的进步使精确度大大提高。早期的模型绘制了对两边300公里网格单元的影响。今天，这一数字降至2.2公里，这使得人们能够详细预测哪些流域和海岸线将最需要保护。
研究人员发现，这些影响远远超出了最初对平均温度的关注。热度记录正在以越来越高的频率被创造出来。降雨模式正在明显转变，超出了自然变化的范围。北极冰层正在以比预期更快的速度缩小。不断上升的海平面已经使一些地方，如威尔士的费尔本，低于大潮水位。
到2050年-英国计划实现碳中性的一年-对英国的直接影响将受到周围海洋的缓解，可能会有机会种植新的作物，但这些好处将因贸易中断、移民、人道主义灾难和生态系统变化而相形见绌。
“总体而言，情况很糟糕。负面因素大于正面因素，“英国气象局科学家理查德·贝茨(Richard Betts)说，他正在领导下一次英国气候变化风险评估的科学分析。“顺理成章的是，如果世界变得越来越热，我们迟早会达到这样的地步，先是不舒服，然后很难发挥作用。英国不会出现这种情况，但在已经炎热潮湿的世界部分地区，天气可能会变得越来越热，无法正常工作。“。
哈德利超级计算机根据进入大气的二氧化碳、甲烷和其他温室气体的数量计算出无数可能的路径。好消息是，最糟糕的排放情景，即RCP8.5，被认为比以前更不可能出现，因为全球煤炭行业并没有像人们担心的那样增长。坏消息是，目前的排放趋势(位于RCP 4.5和RCP 6之间)可能会使地球在本世纪中叶达到比工业化前水平高2摄氏度的温度，这将增加风暴破坏、热浪、海平面上升以及北极夏季无冰的已经很大的风险。
气候趋势基于多年平均值，而不是同比比较，后者更容易受到自然变化的影响。
即使是目前最好的情况-RCP 2.6，大致符合巴黎协议-也会让世界变得比今天更热。
“突出的是，即使在目前最低的情况下，我们也会变暖。我们需要做好准备和适应，“哈德利气候服务部门的负责人杰森·洛(Jason Lowe)说。他预测，像2018年那样的极端夏季热浪将成为常态，而不是例外。
“作为一名科学家，我想缩小不确定性，这样信息就会尽可能好，这样我们就可以做计划了。我想到了未来的几代人。我有一个九岁的女儿。我发现自己在想，当她80岁或90岁时，我们会走上这些道路中的哪一条。“
在光谱的两端都有可能出现更极端的结果。下一套气候评估将引入更雄心勃勃的最好情况(RCP 1.9)，这将意味着更快地过渡到零碳能源，并有更大的机会将气温上升控制在1.5摄氏度以下。但如果气候达到临界点，比如南极西部冰盖崩塌，情况可能会迅速恶化。
斯托特说：“仍然存在我们没有完全了解的危险。”“很大程度上的不确定性在于不利的一面。可能会很可怕。我们能种出足够的庄稼来养活人口吗？如果一些地方一场接一场的暴风雨袭击，我们能应付吗？“。
这些担忧解释了为什么周年纪念不能完全胜利。尽管越来越多的证据表明存在气候风险，但各国政府行动迟缓。除了2008年金融危机和冠状病毒封锁等下行信号外，排放量一直在稳步增加。哈德利中心现在的工作不仅是预测影响，而且是为它们做准备。
“事实证明，我们在二三十年前所说的话是正确的。作为科学家，这就是辩护。但在个人层面上，我希望我们能追踪到与现在不同的排放轨迹。“斯托特说。
“令人担忧的是，我们现在正在全球范围内承担我们没有完全理解的风险……。如果我们继续下去，就不会有气候变化的赢家。科学证据已经存在一段时间了。现在是认真对待它的时候了。“。
从HadISST.2.2.0.0观测到的9月份北极海冰范围(选自气象局气候仪表板的单一来源)。排放情景是气候情景SSP1-2.6(低)、SSP2-4.5(中低)和SSP5-8.5(高)的平均值。
根据美国宇航局卫星数据观测到的全球平均海平面(GMSL)相对于1981-2000年平均水平的变化(以米为单位)。排放情景是来自UKCP18估计的第50个百分位数的预测，RCP2.6(低)，RCP4.5(中低)和RCP8.5(高)。
note_en:
The human fingerprint on the climate is now unmistakable and will become increasingly evident over the coming decades, the UK Met Office has confirmed after 30 years of pioneering study.
 Since the 1990s, global temperatures have warmed by half a degree, Arctic sea ice has shrunk by almost 2 million km2, sea-levels have risen by about 10cm and carbon dioxide in the atmosphere has increased by 60 parts per million (17%), according to figures exclusively compiled for the Guardian to mark the 30th anniversary of the Met Office’s Hadley Centre for climate science and services.
 The data highlights how a young generation has grown up in a climate unprecedented in a millennium. Future projections suggest that by mid-century a 60-year-old Briton is likely to be living in a climate 1.2C warmer than when they were born.
 Scientists at the Hadley Centre, which has been on the global frontline of climate monitoring, research and modelling since it opened in 1990, said early theories about fossil-fuel disruption have been proven by subsequent facts.
 “The climate now is completely different from what we had 30 years ago. It is completely outside the bounds of possibility in natural variation,” said Peter Stott, a professor and expert on climate attribution science at the centre.
  In the Hadley Centre’s early projections, he said, scientists forecast 0.5C of warming in the UK between 1990 and 2020 as a result of emissions from oil, gas and coal: “We got it spot on.”
 With new heat records being broken with increasing frequency, he said global temperatures were now above any level in the Met Office measurements since 1850, or indirectly calculated through tree rings going back thousands of years. Carbon dioxide concentrations in the atmosphere are also higher than anything seen in million-year-old ice cores. “We are seeing an unprecedented climate,” Stott said. “The human fingerprint is everywhere.”
 The impact was less obvious in 1990, when the centre opened in conjunction with the publication of the first report by the UN Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change - both were overseen by the UK scientist John Houghton,  who died earlier this year.
  At the inauguration, the then prime minister, Margaret Thatcher, said the UK needed a world-leading climate centre to assess the “serious consequences” of greenhouse gas emissions. “What it predicts will affect our daily lives. Governments and international organisations in every part of the world are going to have to sit up and take notice and respond,”  she said.
 Thatcher, who studied science at Oxford, needed little convincing, but she had to overcome a sceptical cabinet. Atmospheric physicists  had been warning oil companies and policymakers about the dangers of fossil fuels for decades, but the “greenhouse effect” was still a relatively novel concern for the broader public. Nobody felt a change. The world had already warmed by about half a degree from pre-industrial levels, but this was low enough to be within natural variation.
 Few people knew the difference between short-term “weather” and the “climate”, which is usually measured over a much longer period of 30 years.
 Back then, there was also considerable scepticism about the ability of meteorologists to forecast change, even on a daily basis. Memories were fresh of the BBC weatherman Michael Fish infamously reassuring viewers that fears of a hurricane were unfounded, just hours before the fiercest storm in generations hit the UK.
  “It was unglamorous” recalls James Murphy, a science fellow who was posted to the Hadley Centre from the beginning. “But the mood in the first decade was one of great excitement, because it was a chance to do a lot of pioneering research in a growing field.”
 As acceptance and importance grew, so did scepticism. Critics, often funded by oil firms, pushed back against climate science because they did not like its political and economic implications. Ahead of the climate summits in Kyoto (1997) and Copenhagen (2009), fossil fuel companies funded misinformation campaigns to cast doubt on climate models.
 The Hadley Centre remained focused on science. Researchers expanded and refined their models by incorporating new knowledge on biological carbon cycles and the likely impact of fossil fuels not only on temperature, but on Arctic ice, sea-level rise, flooding, storms, droughts and other atmospheric phenomena.
 Supercomputer advances enabled much greater precision. Early models mapped the impact on grid cells of 300km on each side. Today that is down to 2.2km, which allows detailed predictions of which stretches of river basins and coastlines will need the most protection.
 Researchers are discovering the impacts go far beyond the initial focus on average temperature. Heat records are being set with increasing frequency. Rainfall patterns are noticeably shifting beyond natural variation. Arctic ice is shrinking faster than expected. The rising seas have already left some places, such as  Fairbourne in Wales, below spring-tide levels.
  By 2050 – the year the UK plans to achieve carbon neutrality – the direct impact on Britain will be moderated by the surrounding ocean and there may be opportunities to plant new crops, but these benefits will be dwarfed by trade disruption, migration, humanitarian disasters and shifting ecosystems.
 “Overall it’s bad. The negatives outweigh the positives,” said Richard Betts, a Met Office scientist who is leading scientific analysis for the next UK climate change risk assessment. “It stands to reason that if the world keeps gets hotter and hotter, sooner or later we’ll reach the point where it is first uncomfortable and then hard to function. This won’t be seen in the UK, but in parts of the world that are already hot and humid it could increasingly get too hot to function.”
 The Hadley supercomputer calculates myriad possible pathways depending on how much carbon dioxide, methane and other greenhouse gases enter the atmosphere. The good news is that the worst-emissions scenario, known as RCP 8.5, is considered less likely than before because the global coal industry has not grown as feared. The bad news is current emissions trends (which lie between the RCP 4.5 and RCP 6 pathways) could take the planet to 2C warmer than pre-industrial levels by mid-century, which will increase storm damage, heatwaves, sea-level rise and the already great risk that the Arctic will be ice-free in summer.
 The climate trends are based on multiple-year averages rather than year-on-year comparisons, which are more subject to natural variation.
 Even the current best-case scenario – RCP 2.6, which is roughly in line with the Paris agreement – would leave the world hotter than today.
 “What stands out is that even in the lowest current scenario, we get warming. We’ll need to prepare and adapt,” says Jason Lowe, the head of climate services at Hadley. He predicts extreme summer heatwaves, such as those seen in 2018, will become the norm rather than an exception.
  “As a scientist, I want to narrow the uncertainty so the information is as good as possible so we can plan. I think of the generations to come. I have a nine-year-old daughter. I find myself wondering which of these pathways we will be on when she is 80 or 90.”
 More extreme results are possible at both ends of the spectrum. The next set of climate assessments will introduce a more ambitious best-case scenario (RCP 1.9) that would mean a faster transition to zero-carbon energy and a greater chance of holding temperature rises below 1.5C. But the situation could worsen rapidly if the climate hits tipping points, such as the collapse of the west Antarctic ice sheet.
 “There are still dangers out there that we don’t fully understand,” said Stott. “Much of the uncertainty lies on the bad side. It could be terrifying. Can we grow enough crops to feed the population? Can we cope if some places are battered by storm after storm?”
 Such concerns explain why the anniversary cannot be entirely triumphant. Despite growing evidence of climate risks, governments have been slow to act. Apart from downward blips such as the 2008 financial crisis and the coronavirus lockdown, emissions have steadily increased. The Hadley Centre’s work now is not only about predicting impacts, but preparing for them.
 “Reality has proven that what we were saying 20 to 30 years ago was right. As scientists, that is vindication. But on a personal level, I hoped we would track a different emissions trajectory from where we are now,” Stott said.
 “The worry is that we are now taking risks globally that we don’t fully understand ... there will be no winners of climate change if we continue. Scientific evidence has been around for a while. It is time it was taken seriously.”
  Observed September Arctic sea ice extent from HadISST.2.2.0.0 (selected as single source from  Met Office climate dashboard.) Emission scenarios are the mean averages for climate scenarios SSP1-2.6 (low), SSP2-4.5 (medium-low) and SSP5-8.5 (high).
 Observed changes in global mean sea level (GMSL) in metres from Nasa satellite data relative to 1981-2000 average. Emissions scenarios are 50th percentile projections from UKCP18 estimates, RCP2.6 (low), RCP4.5 (medium-low), and RCP8.5 (high).
title: 来自哈德利超级计算机的汇编数据显示了令人担忧的气候轨迹
title_en: Compiled data from the Hadley's supercomputer shows alarming climate trajectory
